# Welcome {.unnumbered}

This Quarto book serves as a user tutorial for the `fdid` package in R, which implements the **Factorial Difference-in-Differences (FDID)** framework introduced by @xu2026factorial\[<a href="https://yiqingxu.org/papers/2025_fdid/fdid.pdf" target="_blank">Paper</a>\]. This design is tailored for panel data settings where all units are exposed to a universal event but vary in a baseline factor $G$ (currently supported as binary).

`fdid` provides two main functions:

-   `fdid_prepare()`: reshapes long format panel data into a wide `fdid()`-ready format.
-   `fdid()`: performs factorial difference-in-differences estimation using several methods.

And two S3 methods:

-   `summary()`: summarizes the estimation results.
-   `plot()`: visualizes raw means, dynamic effects, and model comparisons.

## Organization

The user tutorial is structured into the following chapters:

-   [Chapter @sec-start]\
    Installation instructions and dataset description.

-   [Chapter @sec-binary]\
    Data preparation, estimation with binary $G$, and basic plots.

-   [Chapter @sec-continuous]\
    Estimation with continuous $G$ using linear methods.

-   [Chapter @sec-sensitivity]\
    Sensitivity analysis for unmeasured confounding using `sensemakr`.

-   [Chapter @sec-visualization]\
    Extended visualization options and method comparisons.

## Contributors

-   [Rivka Lipkovitz](https://rivka.me/){target="_blank"} (MIT)\
-   [Enhan Liu](https://enhan-liu.github.io/){target="_blank"} (UChicago Harris)
-   [Yiqing Xu](https://yiqingxu.org/){target="_blank"} (Stanford)\


## Report bugs

Please report any bugs to me (yiqingxu \[at\] stanford.edu) or submit an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/xuyiqing/fdid/issues). Please include your minimally replicable code and data files and a **panelView** treatment status plot. Your feedback is highly valued!

<!-- badges: start -->

fect: [![Lifecycle: experimental](https://img.shields.io/badge/lifecycle-experimental-orange.svg)](https://www.tidyverse.org/lifecycle/#experimental) [![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT) [<img src="https://cranlogs.r-pkg.org/badges/grand-total/fdid" alt="downloads: CRAN"/>](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/fdid/index.html)

<!-- badges: end -->
